items.title: Fraud detection on imbalanced transaction data
items.scenario: A payments team classifies transactions as fraud or legitimate using amount, merchant category, and device signals. A single deep tree overfits rare fraud patterns. A random forest trains 300 trees on bootstrap samples; each split considers only √p features, so trees explore different fraud signals. Majority vote stabilizes predictions on noisy edge cases.
items.explanation: Bagging plus feature randomness cuts variance on a high-variance base learner—ideal when one tree memorizes rare positives.

items.title: Medical diagnosis from mixed clinical features
items.scenario: A clinic predicts disease risk from lab values, age, and family history. Trees trained on bootstrap resamples capture slightly different patient mixes; random feature subsets prevent every tree from splitting first on age alone. Averaging probability votes yields smoother risk scores for borderline patients.
items.explanation: Feature subsampling decorrelates trees when one variable dominates, improving the benefit of averaging.

items.title: Sensor regression for equipment monitoring
items.scenario: A factory predicts remaining useful life from vibration, temperature, and pressure readings. Each forest tree fits on a bootstrap of historical runs; regression outputs are averaged. OOB rows left out of each bootstrap give a quick generalization estimate without holding back a validation set.
items.explanation: Regression forests average continuous tree outputs; OOB scoring leverages the ~37% of rows absent from each bootstrap sample.
